1.21: vealu moshim behar tziyon lishpot et har Esav vehaitah lADONAI hamelukah: 'Saviors shall go up to Mount Zion to rule Mount Esau, and the kingdom shall be the LORD's.' Obadiah's final verse is the OT's shortest prophetic book's most expansive declaration: the kingdom belongs to YHWH. Edom (the persistent enemy of Israel, descended from Esau) will be judged; the deliverers (moshim, saviors/deliverers) will ascend Zion; the kingdom is YHWH's alone. The NT takes the moshim (saviors/deliverers) as the saints who reign with Christ (Rev 20:4-6) and the kingdom as Christ's eternal reign (Rev 11:15).
